values: September to mid-November (Autumn)
mid-November to February (Winter)
March to April (Spring)
May to June (Summer)
July to September (Monsoon)

evidence.quote_or_summary: Bijli Mahadev in Autumn (September to mid-November)
Bijli Mahadev in Winter (mid-November to February)
Bijli Mahadev in Spring (March to April)
Bijli Mahadev in Summer (May to June)
Bijli Mahadev in Monsoon (July to September)

values: Autumn: clean landscapes, clear skies, vibrant green forest, orange hues by November, villagers preparing for winter
Winter: peaceful and calm atmosphere, light snow dusting on surrounding Pir Panjal range, green deodar forests contrasting with snowy peaks, crisp air
Spring: lush green deodar forests, distant snow-capped Pir Panjal peaks, calm and peaceful trail
Summer: warm but pleasant, cool shaded deodar forest trail, vibrant village life
Monsoon: vibrant green paradise, mist and fog, mystical cloud-covered trails, lush greenery, full streams

evidence.quote_or_summary: Autumn is a great time to trek to Bijli Mahadev. People often associate autumn with fallen leaves and orange hues. However, it is still very green in autumn. It is only when November comes, the landscape starts turning orange.

This is the perfect time for photography because the landscapes are clean and the skies are clear. Villagers are busy getting ready for winter, storing grass for their cattle, and gathering wood, giving trekkers a glimpse into the locals' daily lives.

During the winter season, the Bijli Mahadev trek offers a peaceful and calm atmosphere. While the trail itself doesn't usually have snow, the surrounding mountains, such as the Pir Panjal range, may have a light dusting of snow, creating a beautiful winter scene.
The deodar forests remain green, providing a striking contrast against the snowy peaks.
Spring is a great time to explore the Bijli Mahadev trek. The trail goes through dense deodar forests, which are always lush green, but in spring, the forest feels alive and vibrant.
The distant snow-capped peaks of the Pir Panjal range provide stunning views. The forest is calm in spring, making it a peaceful trek.

In the summer, the trek is warm but pleasant, especially once you enter the dense deodar forest. The shade from the towering trees keeps the trail cool, making it comfortable for walking even in the warmer months.
This season also allows trekkers to experience vibrant village life and get a glimpse of mountain life.
During the monsoon months, the trail becomes a vibrant green paradise. The forest feels alive, and after the rains, everything is fresh and full of life.
If you are fortunate, you might experience trekking under the clouds, with mist and fog adding a mystical touch to the surroundings.

values: January and February: possible snow on surrounding mountains (Pir Panjal range); trail itself usually does not have snow
March: snow may still be present but melts by April

evidence.quote_or_summary: Presence of Snow: You may get snow in January and February
While the trail itself doesn't usually have snow, the surrounding mountains, such as the Pir Panjal range, may have a light dusting of snow
Presence of snow: You might find snow during March but it will have melted by April

values: Monsoon (July to September): trail can be slippery and somewhat challenging due to rain
Landslides and roadblocks more frequent during monsoon, can cause traffic jams and delays en route to base camp
Monsoon treks are usually safe and Indiahikes only runs monsoon treks that are safely navigable in the rains

evidence.quote_or_summary: However, the trail can be slippery and somewhat challenging due to the rain.
Landslides and roadblocks are more frequent during this season, and that can lead to traffic jams and delays en route to the base camp.
Yes, monsoon treks are usually safe. At Indiahikes, we only run those treks in monsoon that are safely navigable in the rains.

values: Autumn (Sep–mid-Nov): daytime 14–18°C, night 8–10°C; 2 warm layers
Winter (mid-Nov–Feb): daytime 12–16°C, night 2–3°C; 4 warm layers
Spring (Mar–Apr): daytime 14–18°C, night 7–10°C; 3–4 warm layers
Summer (May–Jun): daytime 20–24°C, night 12–16°C; 2–3 warm layers
Monsoon (Jul–Sep): daytime 18–22°C, night 12–14°C; 2–3 warm layers (colder if it rains)

evidence.quote_or_summary: Daytime temperatures range from 14°C to 18°C, and at night it can drop to 8°C to 10°C.
Daytime temperatures range from 12°C to 16°C, and at night, they can drop to 2°C to 3°C.
Daytime temperatures range from 14°C to 18°C, and at night, it drops to 7°C to 10°C
During the day, the temperatures range from 20°C to 24°C, while at night, it cools down to 12°C to 16°C.
Daytime temperatures range from 18°C to 22°C, with nighttime temperatures cooling to 12°C to 14°C.

values: Autumn: clean landscapes, clear skies, vibrant green forest turning orange by November
Winter: green deodar forests contrasting with light snow on Pir Panjal peaks
Spring: lush green deodar forests with distant snow-capped Pir Panjal peaks
Summer: shaded deodar forest trail
Monsoon: mist, fog, cloud-covered trails; Pir Panjal range visible when skies clear

evidence.quote_or_summary: This is the perfect time for photography because the landscapes are clean and the skies are clear.
The deodar forests remain green, providing a striking contrast against the snowy peaks.
The distant snow-capped peaks of the Pir Panjal range provide stunning views.
The shade from the towering trees keeps the trail cool
if the skies clear, you can still get great views of the Pir Panjal range.

values: Monsoon: slippery and challenging trail due to rain
Monsoon: landslides and roadblocks en route to base camp causing delays
Monsoon: reach pickup town a day earlier and keep a buffer day after trek
Winter nights drop to 2–3°C requiring 4 warm layers

evidence.quote_or_summary: the trail can be slippery and somewhat challenging due to the rain.
Landslides and roadblocks are more frequent during this season, and that can lead to traffic jams and delays en route to the base camp.
Reach your pickup town a day earlier if possible. It gives you a buffer against unpredictable road conditions. Keep a buffer day after your trek before you plan your return journey.
Daytime temperatures range from 12°C to 16°C, and at night, they can drop to 2°C to 3°C.

values: Autumn: photography enthusiasts wanting clean landscapes and clear skies; those wanting to see mountains clearly
Winter: trekkers wanting winter trekking experience without harsh snow conditions
Spring: beginners wanting Himalayan beauty without extreme conditions
Summer: trekkers wanting to experience mountain village life
Monsoon: trekkers seeking lush greenery, misty trails, and a unique experience

evidence.quote_or_summary: For those who love photography and want to see the mountains clearly, post-monsoon is the best time.
This trek is perfect for those who want to experience winter trekking without facing harsh conditions.
It's perfect for beginners who want to experience the beauty of the Himalayas without extreme conditions.
The cool forest trail provides relief from the summer heat and is the best time to experience mountain village life in action.
The monsoon offers a unique trekking experience, with lush greenery, refreshing rains, and the chance to walk through misty, cloud-covered trails.
